transcript:
[00:03] Jake Hoffman: Thanks for having Mike join today. I wanted to make sure we got alignment at both levels before we move to signatures.
[00:14] Sarah Okonkwo: Mike wanted to hear the commitments directly. He's been burned before.
[00:21] Mike Chen: Let me be blunt. We've signed with three vendors in the last two years who made promises they didn't keep. What makes this different?
[00:33] Jake Hoffman: Fair question. I'll give you three things. One: our API GA date is May 15. That's confirmed with our product team, not estimated.
[00:47] Mike Chen: How confident are you in that date?
[00:52] Jake Hoffman: Very. It's been in QA for three weeks. We've had two clients on early access with no blockers.
[01:05] Sarah Okonkwo: That's what we need. Our Q3 launch depends on it.
[01:12] Jake Hoffman: I understand. And that's why we're also assigning Marcus, our senior onboarding engineer, exclusively to your account for the first 30 days. He'll be the one who does the actual integration work with your team.
[01:28] Mike Chen: What's his background?
[01:33] Jake Hoffman: Six years in DevOps, former Stripe engineer. He's done Node.js migrations before — which I know is your stack.
[01:45] Sarah Okonkwo: That's actually reassuring. One of my concerns was that we'd get handed to a generic onboarding team.
[01:55] Jake Hoffman: We won't do that to a deal this size.
[02:05] Mike Chen: What about security? We're fintech. SOC 2 is non-negotiable.
[02:12] Jake Hoffman: We're SOC 2 Type II certified. I'll send you the report and our most recent pen test results with the contract.
[02:22] Sarah Okonkwo: We'll want our security team to review before we go live, but that shouldn't be a blocker if everything checks out.
[02:33] Jake Hoffman: Understood. We've done this process with three other fintechs in the last year — we can usually turn that review around in a week.
[02:45] Mike Chen: What happens if the API slips past May 15?
[02:52] Jake Hoffman: That's fair to ask. If we miss May 15, we'll give you a 30-day extension on your contract at no charge. But I don't expect that to happen.
[03:08] Sarah Okonkwo: I appreciate that. One thing I want to flag — James, our Senior DevOps, wasn't in any of our calls. He's the one who will actually do the integration work. We need to get him involved early.
[03:24] Jake Hoffman: Let's put him on the kickoff. I'll ask Marcus to reach out to him directly before we start so they can connect engineer-to-engineer.
[03:36] Sarah Okonkwo: Perfect. That will make James a lot more comfortable.
[03:44] Mike Chen: What's the payment structure?
[03:50] Jake Hoffman: Milestone-based. 50% at kickoff, 50% when API is live and you've confirmed it's working in your environment.
[04:02] Mike Chen: That's reasonable. If the API never goes live, we don't pay the second half.
[04:10] Jake Hoffman: Correct. It's tied to delivery, not time.
[04:18] Sarah Okonkwo: We're ready to move forward. We've evaluated DataBridge and NovaSuite — you're the right call for our stack and our timeline.
[04:30] Mike Chen: Agreed. But I want a written record of everything promised today. The API date, Marcus, the security review timeline, the milestone payments.
[04:43] Jake Hoffman: I'll have a deal summary document in your inbox by end of day. Everything we discussed, in writing.
[04:52] Sarah Okonkwo: That's all we needed. Let's do this.
[04:58] Jake Hoffman: Looking forward to it. Your CSM, Sarah Johnson, will be reaching out within 24 hours to kick things off.
